steps.description: Click the "Create a new app" button located in the main content area to initiate the process of creating and registering a new app in Microsoft 365.

Type text: '${{var:app_name:tdp_app_#####}}'  into the 'Name' input field within the 'Create a new app' dialog box in the 'Apps' section of the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal interface.And input ${{var:app_name:tdp_app_#####}} into the run time variable.
Click the "Create" button in the "Create a new app" dialog to initiate app creation with the provided Name and Manifest version fields.
Click the "App features" link in the navigation menu on the left side of the "Apps: Developer Portal" dashboard interface.
Click the "Bot" feature card under the "App features" section in the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal interface.
Click the dropdown menu labeled "Select an existing bot" in the "Identify your bot" section of the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal interface.
Click the "vscuse_app_3Pxjp (a7f38049-3bc1-41d6-bc79-b65fffdfc602)" bot entry in the list within the "Apps: Developer Portal" interface to select it.
Click the "Save" button at the bottom-right corner of the "Identify your bot" configuration section in the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal interface.
Click on the "App features" navigation item in the left-hand sidebar within the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal interface.
Press the "End" key to navigate to the bottom of the "Dashboard" section within the Developer Portal interface.
Press the "End" key to scroll to the bottom of the "App features" configuration page in the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal.
Click on the "Open in Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" option located under the "Develop" section in the left sidebar menu within the Developer Portal interface.
Click the "Open in Visual Studio Code (JS/TS)" button located in the "Develop" section of the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal interface.
Click the "Open Visual Studio Code - URL Handler" button in a browser permission prompt requesting to open a URL in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Open" button in the confirmation dialog titled "Allow 'Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit' extension to open this URI?" displayed within the Visual Studio Code application.
Click on the "Sign in" button in the Microsoft 365 login prompt to proceed with authentication in the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "Use weaker encryption" button in the dialog box titled "An OS keyring couldn't be identified for storing the encryption-related data in your current desktop environment."
Type text Type text "${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD}}" into the password input field on the Microsoft account login page.
Press the "Enter" key to submit the entered password and proceed from the Microsoft login screen's "Sign in" button.
Click the close ("X") button at the top-right corner of the browser window to close the "M365 Account - Sign In" webpage.
@assertion 'Create new bot(s) for debugging' is exist
Click the checkbox labeled "Deselect to keep the original botId value" in the dropdown menu of Visual Studio Code.
Click the "TypeScript" option in the "Programming Language" dropdown menu.
Click on the "Default folder" text field within the "Workspace Folder" dialog to select or modify the path `/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects`.
Press the 'Enter' key to confirm the application name entered in the "Input an application name" text box within the Visual Studio Code interface.
@assertion '${{var:app_name:tdp_app_#####}}' is exist
